title: A stranger who helped me once
summary: Tell a short story about a stranger who helped you on one ordinary day.
topic: A stranger who helped me once
prompt: Write 6–8 short sentences about one time a stranger helped you. Say where you were, what the problem was, what the stranger did, and how you felt. Use past simple.
hintStarters: One cold evening in Lisbon, I…
I did not know the street and…
An old woman saw me and…
sampleText: One cold evening in Lisbon, I walked home from a small restaurant. The streets were narrow and dark, and I did not know the way. My phone battery was dead, so I could not use a map. I was a little scared. An old woman stood in front of her door. She saw my face and she smiled. I asked her about my hotel in slow English. She did not speak English well, but she understood. She put on her coat and she walked with me. We walked for ten minutes through quiet streets. She pointed at the tram stop and at the corner of my hotel. I thanked her many times. She just touched my arm and said one short word. I did not learn her name. She turned around and she walked home alone. I stood on the street and I watched her go. I felt safe and very thankful. A stranger was kind to me on a cold night. I still remember her face.

sampleAnnotations.meaningVi: cảm ơn bà rất nhiều lần
cảm thấy an toàn và rất biết ơn

sampleGrammarNotes.title: Past simple for a short story
sampleGrammarNotes.bodyVi: Kể chuyện trong quá khứ dùng thì quá khứ đơn: 'walked', 'saw', 'smiled', 'asked'. Động từ bất quy tắc: see → saw, stand → stood, understand → understood.
sampleGrammarNotes.bodyEn: Use past simple to tell a past story: 'walked', 'saw', 'smiled', 'asked'. Watch irregular verbs: see → saw, stand → stood, understand → understood.

sampleGrammarNotes.title: Linkers 'so' and 'but'
sampleGrammarNotes.bodyVi: 'so' giới thiệu kết quả: 'My battery was dead, so I could not use a map'. 'but' giới thiệu sự đối lập: 'She did not speak English well, but she understood'.
sampleGrammarNotes.bodyEn: 'so' introduces a result: 'My battery was dead, so I could not use a map'. 'but' introduces a contrast: 'She did not speak English well, but she understood'.

sampleTranslationVi: Một buổi tối lạnh ở Lisbon, tôi đi bộ về từ một nhà hàng nhỏ. Các con phố hẹp và tối, và tôi không biết đường. Điện thoại của tôi hết pin, nên tôi không thể dùng bản đồ. Tôi hơi sợ. Một bà cụ đứng trước cửa nhà mình. Bà nhìn mặt tôi và mỉm cười. Tôi hỏi bà về khách sạn của mình bằng tiếng Anh chậm. Bà không nói tiếng Anh giỏi, nhưng bà hiểu. Bà khoác áo và đi bộ cùng tôi. Chúng tôi đi khoảng mười phút qua những con phố yên tĩnh. Bà chỉ vào trạm xe điện và góc khách sạn của tôi. Tôi cảm ơn bà rất nhiều lần. Bà chỉ chạm vào tay tôi và nói một từ ngắn. Tôi không biết tên bà. Bà quay lưng và đi về nhà một mình. Tôi đứng trên phố và nhìn bà đi. Tôi cảm thấy an toàn và rất biết ơn. Một người lạ đã tử tế với tôi vào một đêm lạnh. Tôi vẫn nhớ khuôn mặt bà.

mcQuestions.explanation: The text says 'One cold evening in Lisbon'.

mcQuestions.prompt: Why could the writer not use a map?
options: He did not have a phone.
mcQuestions.explanation: The text says 'My phone battery was dead, so I could not use a map'.
mcQuestions.hint: Look for the sentence about the phone.

mcQuestions.explanation: The text says 'An old woman stood in front of her door'.
mcQuestions.hint: Look for the person who saw the writer's face.

mcQuestions.prompt: How did the writer speak to her?
mcQuestions.explanation: The text says 'I asked her about my hotel in slow English'.
mcQuestions.hint: Find the part about how the writer asked.

mcQuestions.prompt: What did the old woman do for the writer?
options: She walked with him to the tram stop and his hotel.
mcQuestions.explanation: The text says she 'walked with me' and 'pointed at the tram stop and at the corner of my hotel'.
mcQuestions.hint: Find what she did after she put on her coat.

mcQuestions.prompt: Which past simple form is correct?
mcQuestions.hint: Regular verbs add -ed in the past.

mcQuestions.prompt: Choose the correct past simple of 'see'.

options: My phone was dead, so I could not use a map.
My phone was dead so map I no.
So my phone dead, map I could not.
My phone so was dead I no map.
mcQuestions.explanation: 'so' joins a cause and its result: dead phone → no map.

mcQuestions.prompt: How did the writer feel at the end?
mcQuestions.explanation: The text says 'I felt safe and very thankful'.
mcQuestions.hint: Find the feelings sentence near the end.

mcQuestions.prompt: Which sentence uses 'but' to show a contrast?
options: She did not speak English well, but she understood.
She did not speak English but well understood.
She but did not speak English well understood.
But she did not understood English speak.
mcQuestions.explanation: 'but' joins two contrasting ideas: weak English, yet she understood.
mcQuestions.hint: Look for two ideas that seem to go against each other.

criticalThinkingQuestion: Why do we sometimes remember kind strangers more clearly than people we see every day?
